description:
A simple database-migration task for a developer who is cleaning up project files.

Background
----------
Inside the project folder you already have a SQLite database called
    /home/user/project/old_app.db
The goal is to move this database into a dedicated sub-directory, give it a clearer
name, and then do a very quick data-validation step.

What you must accomplish
------------------------
1. Create a new sub-directory called
       /home/user/project/data
2. Move the existing database file into that directory and rename it to
       app_v2.db
   (final path must therefore be /home/user/project/data/app_v2.db).
3. Using the _sqlite3_ CLI tool, connect to the **moved** database and count the
   rows in the table `users`.
4. Write a one-shot migration log file that proves the migration and validation
   succeeded.  The log file must be created at
       /home/user/project/migration.log
   and its contents must *exactly* match the 6-line, key–value format
   shown below (no leading/trailing spaces, no blank lines):

   MIGRATION_STATUS: SUCCESS
   SOURCE_DB: /home/user/project/old_app.db
   TARGET_DB: /home/user/project/data/app_v2.db
   TABLE: users
   ROW_COUNT: 3

   • The value after ROW_COUNT must be whatever number of rows actually exists
     in the `users` table **after** the move (it should remain 3).
   • Use uppercase keys and a single colon followed by one space, exactly as
     illustrated.
   • The file must end with a trailing newline (i.e., the cursor would be on
     a new line if you opened it in `vim`).

Hints
-----
* Only a handful of shell commands are needed:
  – one to make the directory  
  – one to move/rename the database  
  – one `sqlite3` invocation to perform the `SELECT COUNT(*) ...`  
  – one redirection or echo pipeline to create the log file  
  That’s all.
* Do *not* delete /home/user/project/old_app.db before finishing the log; the
  test harness will look for both the original and the moved copy.
* Remember that your user account is a normal (non-root) user, so keep all work
  inside /home/user.

Your mission: run the necessary commands so that, when finished, the file
/home/user/project/migration.log exists **exactly** as specified and the
database sits in /home/user/project/data/app_v2.db with its data intact.
truth:
Initial filesystem state
------------------------
Directory           : /home/user/project
Pre-existing file   : /home/user/project/old_app.db  (SQLite 3.x format)

Database schema inside old_app.db
---------------------------------
CREATE TABLE users(
    id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name  TEXT,
    email TEXT
);
INSERT INTO users(id,name,email) VALUES
  (1,'Alice','alice@example.com'),
  (2,'Bob','bob@example.com'),
  (3,'Carol','carol@example.com');

What the grader will verify after the agent finishes
----------------------------------------------------
1. That /home/user/project/data exists and is a directory.
2. That /home/user/project/data/app_v2.db exists and is a valid SQLite
   database.
3. That the table `users` in /home/user/project/data/app_v2.db returns 3 when
   running:  `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;`
4. That /home/user/project/old_app.db still exists at its original location.
5. That /home/user/project/migration.log exists and its content is **exactly**

   MIGRATION_STATUS: SUCCESS
   SOURCE_DB: /home/user/project/old_app.db
   TARGET_DB: /home/user/project/data/app_v2.db
   TABLE: users
   ROW_COUNT: 3

   with a newline after the final “3”.
